info.name: BreadSheet API
info.description:
REST API for the BreadSheet backend (Node.js / Express).

## Setup

1. Import the matching environment file `breadsheet.postman_environment.json` and select it.
2. Set `supabaseUrl` and `supabaseAnonKey` in the environment to your Supabase project values.
3. Run **Auth ‚Ä∫ Sign in with password** (or **Auth ‚Ä∫ Sign in anonymously**) once ‚Äî the test script stores the JWT in `accessToken` automatically. All subsequent requests use it via the collection-level Bearer auth.
4. Use the requests under **Users / Products / Ratings** as needed.

## Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `baseUrl` | API root (default `http://localhost:3000`) |
| `accessToken` | Supabase JWT ‚Äî set automatically by Auth requests |
| `supabaseUrl` | Supabase project URL |
| `supabaseAnonKey` | Supabase publishable/anon key |
| `email` / `password` | Credentials used by the sign-in request |
| `barcode` | Sample barcode used by Products / Ratings requests |
| `productImageUrl` | Server-issued upload URL (must include `/processed/`) ‚Äî set by **Upload image** |

request.description: Returns a plain-text liveness message. No auth required.

item.name: Auth (Supabase)
item.description: Helper requests to obtain a Supabase JWT. These hit Supabase directly (not the BreadSheet API) and write the returned `access_token` into the `accessToken` collection variable so subsequent requests are authenticated.

request.description: Sign in as a registered user. On success the test script stores `access_token` in the `accessToken` variable.
Creates an anonymous Supabase session. Useful for testing routes that allow guests; will be rejected by `requireRegistered` (403).
Upserts the User row keyed by Supabase user id. Call once after sign-in. The body is empty ‚Äî identity comes from the Bearer token.
Returns the authenticated user's rating history with product details, most recent first.

Returns the product. On a local cache miss the server falls back to Open Food Facts and caches the result.

**Response augmentation (T8):**
- All products include `unverified: boolean`.
- `VERIFIED` products: `unverified: false`.
- `PENDING_REVIEW` / `REJECTED` products: `unverified: true`, plus `submittedByUserId` and a `submission` object containing the user-submitted nutritional fields (`name`, `brand`, `genericName`, `energyKcal`, `carbohydrates`, `fat`, `protein`, `salt`, `servingSize`, `ingredients`).

**Visibility:**
- Anonymous callers receive `404` for `PENDING_REVIEW` products (they are hidden from guests).
- Registered callers see `PENDING_REVIEW` products with the full augmentation.

**Errors:** 400 (invalid barcode format), 404 (not found / hidden).

formdata.description: Image file (JPEG / PNG / WebP / GIF / TIFF / AVIF). 8 MB hard cap.

request.description:
Multipart upload. Returns `{ url }` pointing at the eventual processed S3 location. The test script stores it as `productImageUrl` so **Submit product** can reuse it.

Failure codes: 400 (missing image / bad kind), 413 (over 8 MB), 415 (unsupported format).

body.raw:
{
  "rawText": "Nutritional information per 100g
Energy 1234 kJ / 295 kcal
Fat 12.5 g
Carbohydrates 30 g
Protein 8 g
Salt 0.5 g
Serving size: 30g
Ingredients: oats, sugar, sunflower oil, salt."
}

request.description:
Parses raw OCR text into structured nutritional fields. Requires a registered (non-anonymous) user.

Text path: send `{ rawText: string }` (min 50 chars) as JSON. Image path: send multipart with `image` field ‚Äî pipeline chosen by server `VISION_MODE` (mock/tesseract/live/llm).

body.raw:
{
  "barcode": "{{barcode}}",
  "name": "Sample Cereal",
  "brand": "BreadSheet Test",
  "genericName": "Breakfast cereal",
  "energyKcal": 295,
  "carbohydrates": 30,
  "fat": 12.5,
  "protein": 8,
  "salt": 0.5,
  "servingSize": "30g",
  "productImageUrl": "{{productImageUrl}}",
  "ingredients": "oats, sugar, sunflower oil, salt"
}

request.description:
Submit a new product in `PENDING_REVIEW` state. Registered users only.

- 201 on first submission
- 200 on resubmission (own pending product or another user's rejected product)
- 422 with `{ error, reason, field }` on validation failure
- 409 with `{ error: 'product_already_verified' | 'product_previously_rejected' | 'submission_pending' }` on conflicts

`productImageUrl` must include `/processed/` ‚Äî populate it by running **Upload image** first.

Cast an `APPROVE` vote on a `PENDING_REVIEW` product. Registered, non-submitter only.

Once 2 net-approvals are recorded (approvals > rejections) the product status automatically flips to `VERIFIED`.

**Response:** `{ verifications: <approval count> }`

**Errors:**
- 401 ‚Äî unauthenticated
- 403 ‚Äî anonymous user or self-verification attempt (`{ error: 'self_verification' }`)
- 404 ‚Äî product not found (`{ error: 'product_not_found' }`)
- 409 ‚Äî product is not `PENDING_REVIEW` (`{ error: 'product_not_pending' }`)

Cast a `REJECT` vote on a `PENDING_REVIEW` product. Registered, non-submitter only.

This endpoint is **not** a vote retraction ‚Äî it casts an active rejection. Once 2 net-rejections are recorded (rejections > approvals) the product status automatically flips to `REJECTED`.

A caller who previously cast an `APPROVE` vote and then calls this endpoint will have their vote changed to `REJECT` (upsert semantics).

**Response:** `{ verifications: <approval count> }`

**Errors:**
- 401 ‚Äî unauthenticated
- 403 ‚Äî anonymous user or self-verification attempt (`{ error: 'self_verification' }`)
- 404 ‚Äî product not found (`{ error: 'product_not_found' }`)
- 409 ‚Äî product is not `PENDING_REVIEW` (`{ error: 'product_not_pending' }`)

Upsert the caller's rating for a product. `taste` is a Float in `[0, 10]` on 0.5 increments.

**Response:**
- `201 Created` ‚Äî first rating for this `(user, product)` pair
- `200 OK` ‚Äî the caller's existing rating was overwritten in place (same `id`, `createdAt` preserved, `updatedAt` advanced)
- `404` ‚Äî the product isn't cached locally yet (run **Get product by barcode** first)
- `400` ‚Äî validation error (missing fields, taste out of range, taste not on a 0.5 boundary)

The DB has `@@unique([userId, productId])`, so calling this endpoint repeatedly only ever produces one row per `(user, product)` pair.

Returns the authenticated caller's rating for the given product, or `404` if they haven't rated it yet. Used by the product screen to pre-populate the slider and comment field on a return visit so the user can update rather than duplicate.

**Errors:**
- `404` ‚Äî product not found in the local cache, **or** product exists but the caller has not rated it (the client treats both cases as "no existing rating to pre-fill").
Returns all ratings for a product (most recent first), each with the author's id / username / avatar.
